q: Sound is a sequence of ___ waves which propagates through compressible media such as air or water.
During their propagation, waves can be reflected, refracted or ___ by the medium.
A relationship between density and ___, determines the speed of sound within the medium.
This relationship, affected by ___, determines the speed of sound within the medium.
The ___ of the medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated.
For many media, such as air or water, attenuation due to viscosity is ___.
Speed of a sound wave can also be affected by the density, temperature, and ___ of the medium through which the sound waves travel.
Hence, the speed of sound will be ___ for different media.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, where $Y$ is ___.
In liquids, the velocity of sound is given by a similar expression, $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, where $B$ is the ___.
In gases, the velocity of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}}$, where $\gamma$ is the ___.
Longitudinal sound waves travel ___ in solids than they do in liquids than they do in gases.
Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___ properties of the medium.
The SI units of Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___.
The level of intensity of a sound is measured relative to a ___ value $I_o$.
The reference intensity value corresponds to the minimum intensity of sound that can be heard (___).
The ___ is the unit used for measuring sound intensity.
For every $10 \text{ dB}$ added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by ___ times.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the ___ reaching an eardrum measured in $\text{W m}^{-2}$.
Echoes are produced where a sound wave is reflected from a large and ___ surface.
An echo is repetition of sound due to the ___ of sound.
The echo or the reflected sound can be heard separately from the original sound if the source of the sound is much ___ to the observer than to the reflecting surface.
Reflection of sound obeys the laws of ___.
To reduce the effect of echo in buildings, walls are covered up with ___.
These materials ___ the incident sound and reduce the amount of sound reflecting off wall and floor surfaces.
Echo can also be used to determine the ___ $d$ between a source of sound and the reflecting surface.
Some animals such as bats are known to use ___ to navigate and locate their prey.
Echolocation is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the ___.
A ___ is a reflection of light or sound waves, or a wide reaching impact of an action.
An example of a reverberation is the sound ___ around in a large speaker.
___ means able to be heard.
Audibility means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in certain range of ___.
The human's ear is capable of detecting sounds only in a certain range of frequencies known as the range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit is $20 \text{ Hz}$ and the upper limit is about ___.
By using ___ generator, the audible frequency range can be determined.
Low frequency sounds which are under the lower limit of audibility ($20 \text{ Hz}$) are called ___.
A vibrating ruler can be seen but not heard its vibrating sound because the sound produced is ___ the lower limit of audibility of human ear.
High frequency sounds which are above the upper limit of audibility ($20\ 000 \text{ Hz}$) are called ___.
Bats and whales use ultrasound in echolocation and most ___ technologies use ultrasound.
Manufacturers of concrete use ultrasound to check for cracks or ___.
A prenatal ultrasound test uses high-frequency sound waves, inaudible to the human ear, that are transmitted through the abdomen via a device called a ___.
With prenatal ultrasound, the echoes are recorded and transformed into video or ___ images of the baby.
Ultrasonic scalers use ultrasound to remove calculus (___) deposits from the teeth.
The transmission of high energy ultrasound may result in the certain of ___ bubbles.
Cavitation bubbles are created at sites of ___.
Ultrasonic cleaning is especially effective in the cleaning of irregular surfaces or internal ___ and passageways.
The sonar (sound navigation and ranging) is a type of technology that works based on ___.
It is used by the ships for navigation at sea and to detect the ___ of other vessels.
Infrasound waves have long ___, and are thus able to travel over long distances.
Infrasound waves which have lower frequency and longer wavelengths are able to travel longer distances with less ___.
The Doppler effect or Doppler shift is the change in ___ of a wave related to an observer.
It is due to the ___ motion between the source and observer.
It is named after the Austrian physicist ___ Doppler.
A common example of Doppler shift is the change of frequency (___) heard when a vehicle sounding a horn approach and recedes from an observer.
The change of frequency heard when a vehicle sounding a horn approaches and recedes from an observer is a common example of ___.
The general Doppler equation is $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$, where $f_o$ is the frequency heard by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $f_s$ represents the frequency produced by the ___.
When the observer moves away from the source, the sign used for $v_o$ is ___.
When the source moves away from the observer, the sign used for $v_s$ is ___.
The wavelength of light from a star shifts towards the red end of the spectrum when the earth and the star are moving ___ from each other.
Radar stands for radio detection and ___.
Doppler ultrasound is a technique that can reveal blockage to blood flow and show the formation of ___ in arteries.
The pitch of the note is determined by its ___.
The greater the frequency, the higher the ___.
The loudness of a note depends upon the ___ of the wave that produces it.
Quality of a sound is the characteristic which distinguishes the two sounds of the same loudness and same pitch but emitted by two different ___.
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___.
The intensity of the sound at a place is the energy transmitted per second per unit area held normally to the direction of ___.
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the square of the ___ of the wave.
Loudness is a ___ and hence, unlike intensity, it depends on the observer.
The formula for intensity is $I = \frac{1}{2} \rho \omega^2 A^2$, where $\rho$ is the density of the ___.
The Doppler effect is used in police radar, air traffic control radar, and ___ radar.
The frequency shift in Doppler ultrasound used to examine blood flow is called ___.
When the source moves towards the observer, the sign used for $v_s$ in the Doppler equation is ___.
When the observer moves towards the source, the sign used for $v_o$ in the Doppler equation is ___.
Notes produced by a violin sound richer than others because it has more ___.
The intensity of a wave is proportional to the square of the angular frequency, denoted by the symbol ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v$ stands for the speed of ___ in the medium.
The fractional change in wavelength $\frac{\Delta \lambda}{\lambda}$ is equal to $\frac{v}{c}$ for light moving away, where $c$ is the speed of ___.
The ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in a certain range of frequencies is called ___.
The Doppler effect is used to provide detailed information about the heartbeat of the ___ during labor and delivery.
Radar uses electromagnetic waves instead of ___ waves.
The amplitude of the wave is denoted by the variable ___.
The timbre of a sound varies with the number of overtones or ___.
When observer and source are stationary, the values of $v_o$ and $v_s$ are equal to ___.
The Doppler equation is expressed in general form as $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$, where $f_s$ is the frequency produced by the ___.
The intensity of sound at a place is energy transmitted per second per unit ___.
The sound of a violin differs from a tuning fork due to its ___.
Loudness is subjective, meaning it is a ___.
In the red shift phenomenon, light shifts towards the red end of the spectrum, which corresponds to lower frequency and ___ wavelength.
The Doppler effect can be used to examine organs such as the heart and ___.
Sound intensity is defined as energy transmitted per ___ per unit area.
The timbre of a sound depends on its waveform, which varies with the number of harmonics or ___.
The intensity of a wave $I$ is directly proportional to the square of the amplitude $A^2$ and the square of the ___ $\omega^2$.
